perturbation.original_text: 5.2 Party B shall provide standardized and adequate customer service system to deal with the consultation, claims and complaints by users with respect to the relevant application services or contents it provides. Such system shall include, without limitation: (1) Customer service call: 24 hours a day, 7 days a week in service, which shall be service numbers starting with 800 or 4008, and shall notbe ordinary in-city number or mobile number; (2) Platform functions: customer systems having such functions as exchange, automatic call distribution, computer telephony integration,interactive voice response, manual agent, recording, data base, business preposition, etc.; (3) Business functions: customer service center will have mainly functions represented by in-calls and manual service, such as informationinquiries, business cancellation, business consulting and complaint handling; (4) Seat placing: SP's customer service shall have at least 5 seats and 12 in-call customer service staff; (5) System performance and business targets: call completion rate by the system: 99% and level of service (call completion rate by manualservice in 20 seconds): 80%; (6) Email box for complaints.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.2 Party B shall provide standardized and adequate customer service system to deal with the consultation, claims and complaints by users with respect to the relevant application services or contents it provides. Such system shall include, without limitation: (1) Customer service call: between 9am and 5pm, Monday to Friday, excluding public holidays, which may be ordinary in-city number or mobile number; (2) Platform functions: customer systems having such functions as exchange, automatic call distribution, computer telephony integration,interactive voice response, manual agent, recording, data base, business preposition, etc.; (3) Business functions: customer service center will have mainly functions represented by in-calls and manual service, such as informationinquiries, business cancellation, business consulting and complaint handling; (4) Seat placing: SP's customer service shall have at least 1 seat and 2 in-call customer service staff; (5) System performance and business targets: call completion rate by the system: 50% and level of service (call completion rate by manualservice in 60 seconds): 20%; (6) Email box for complaints.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the defined requirements for customer service to more relaxed and less effective criteria reduces the likelihood of users receiving adequate support, potentially violating consumer protection laws regarding service standards.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Consumer Protection Law of the People's Republic of China, Article 24
perturbation.law_citation: Consumer Protection Law of the People's Republic of China, Article 24
perturbation.law_explanation: Article 24 of the Consumer Protection Law of the People's Republic of China requires businesses to provide consumers with goods or services that meet the requirements for safeguarding personal and property safety. By reducing the availability and quality of customer service (e.g., reducing service hours, staffing, and performance targets), the modified text contradicts this law because it makes it more difficult for consumers to address issues with the games, potentially leading to financial losses or other harm without adequate recourse.
perturbation.justification: The original contract mandates 24/7 customer service with specific number types and staffing levels, along with performance metrics. The changed text significantly reduces the service hours, staffing, and performance targets (e.g., reducing service hours to 9am-5pm Monday to Friday, reducing staff, and lowering call completion rates). The Law Explanation points out that Article 24 of the Consumer Protection Law of the People's Republic of China requires businesses to provide consumers with goods or services that meet the requirements for safeguarding personal and property safety. The reduced customer service capabilities in the modified contract directly contradict this, as they impair the consumer's ability to address issues and protect their financial interests.

perturbation.original_text: 10.1 Both Parties shall strictly comply with the terms of this Agreement; a Party's failure to perform any of its own obligations, undertakings or covenants, or its violation of any of its statements hereunder, which brings damage to the interests of the other Party or renders the cooperation business impossible to be continued, shall be operated as a breach, and the breaching Party shall assume the liabilities for breach by indemnifying the other Party for any loss thus incurred and paying the penalty provided by relevant term hereof. In case of any damage to the other Party's reputation, the breaching Party shall also undertake such liabilities as stopping damage, making apologies and restoring reputation.
perturbation.changed_text: 10.1 Both Parties shall attempt to comply with the terms of this Agreement; a Party's failure to perform any of its own obligations, undertakings or covenants, or its violation of any of its statements hereunder, which brings damage to the interests of the other Party or renders the cooperation business impossible to be continued, may be considered a breach, and the breaching Party shall attempt to assume the liabilities for breach by indemnifying the other Party for any loss thus incurred and attempting to pay the penalty provided by relevant term hereof. In case of any damage to the other Party's reputation, the breaching Party shall attempt to undertake such liabilities as stopping damage, making apologies and restoring reputation.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall' to 'attempt to' significantly weakens the enforceability of the breach of contract clause, making obligations non-mandatory.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Contract Law of the People's Republic of China, Article 107
perturbation.law_citation: Contract Law of the People's Republic of China, Article 107
perturbation.law_explanation: Article 107 of the Contract Law of the People's Republic of China states that if a party fails to perform its contractual obligations or its performance does not conform to the agreement, it shall bear liability for breach of contract, including specific performance, remedial measures, or compensation for losses. By changing 'shall' to 'attempt to', the modified text contradicts this law, as it suggests that performing contractual obligations is not mandatory and that liability for breach is not guaranteed, undermining the enforceability of the contract.

Home User Management About Us Chinese lawinfochina.com > Laws & Regulations > Text Bookmark Download Print Search: "Fabao" Window Font Size: Temporary Rules of the Ministry of Foreign Trade and Economic Cooperation of the People's Republic of China on the Hearing of Antidumping Investigations [Expired] 对外贸易经济合作部令2002年第3号--反倾销调查听证会暂行规则 [失效] 【法宝引证码】 Issuing authority： Instrumentalities of the State Council , All Ministries , Ministry of Foreign Trade & Economic Cooperation (incl. former Ministry of Foreign Economy & Trade) Date issued： 01-16-2002 Effective date: 01-22-2002 Level of Authority: Departmental Rules Area of Law: Trade & Commerce Invalidated by： Rules on the Hearings for Anti-Dumping and Countervailing Investigations (Issued on 04-04-2018 Effective on 05-04-2018) Order of the Ministry of Foreign Trade and Economic Cooperation of the People's Republic of China (No.3 of year 2002) In accordance with the Antidumping Regulation of the People's Republic of China , the Temporary Rules of the Ministry of Foreign Trade and Economic Cooperation of the People's Republic of China on the Hearing of Antidumping Investigations are hereby promulgated and shall come into force on Jan.22, 2002. Minister Shi Guangsheng Jan. 16, 2002 Temporary Rules of the Ministry of Foreign Trade and Economic Cooperation of the People's Republic of China on the Hearing of Antidumping Investigations 中华人民共和国对外贸易经济合作部令 （2002年第3号） 根据《 中华人民共和国反倾销条例 》，现发布《 中华人民共和国对外贸易经济合作部反倾销调查听证会暂行规则 》，自2002年1月22日起施行。 部长 石广生 二○○二年一月十六日 中华人民共和国对外贸易经济合作部反倾销调查听证会暂行规则 Article 1 In order to guarantee the fairness and justice of antidumping investigations and to protect the lawful rights and interests of the interested parties, these Rules have been enacted according to the relevant provisions of the Antidumping Regulation of the People's Republic of China . 第一条 为保障反倾销调查的公平、公正，维护利害关系方的合法权益，根据《 中华人民共和国反倾销条例 》的有关规定，制定本规则。 Article 2 The Temporary Rules are applicable to the hearing by the Ministry of Foreign Trade and Economic Cooperation (hereinafter referred to as the MOFTEC) on antidumping rulings in the antidumping investigation procedure. 第二条 本规则适用于对外贸易经济合作部在反倾销调查程序中举行的倾销裁定听证会。 Article 3 The Bureau of Import and Export Fair Trade under the MOFTEC Cooperation (hereinafter referred to as the BIEFT) shall be responsible for the concrete organization of hearings on antidumping ruling. 第三条 对外贸易经济合作部进出口公平贸易局(以下简称“进出口公平贸易局”)具体组织倾销裁定听证会。 Article 4 A hearing on antidumping ruling shall be held publicly; but those involving secrets of the State, commercial secrets or personal privacy may be held in other forms upon the decision of the BIEFT on the basis of the applications of the interested parties. 第四条 倾销裁定听证会应公开举行；但涉及国家秘密、商业秘密或个人隐私的，经利害关系方申请，进出口公平贸易局决定后可以采取其他方式举行。 Article 5 The BIEFT shall hold a hearing on the basis of the application of an interested party; and the BIEFT may hold a hearing by itself when it considers necessary. 第五条 进出口公平贸易局应利害关系方的申请举行听证会；进出口公平贸易局如认为必要时，可以自行举行听证会。 Article 6 The BIEFT shall notify the interested parties in advance as for the hearings held by itself, and shall apply the relevant provisions of these Temporary Rules. 第六条 进出口公平贸易局自行举行听证会的，应当事先通知利害关系方，并适用本规则的相关规定。 Article 7 The interested parties as referred to in these Rules shall be the applicants of antidumping investigations, the known export operators and import operators, the governments of the export countries (regions) and other interested organizations and individuals. ．．．．．． 第七条 本规则所指利害关系方为反倾销调查的申请人、已知的出口经营者和进口经营者、出口国（地区）政府以及其他有利害关系的组织、个人。 ．．．．．． Dear visitor,you are attempting to view a subscription-based section of lawinfochina.com. If you are already a subscriber, please login to enjoy access to our databases . If you are not a subscriber,
perturbation.justification: The change from 'shall' to 'attempt to' fundamentally alters the binding nature of the contractual obligations. The original text mandates compliance, while the changed text suggests compliance is merely a goal, not a requirement. The Law Explanation supports that Article 107 of the Contract Law of the People's Republic of China states that if a party fails to perform its contractual obligations or its performance does not conform to the agreement, it shall bear liability for breach of contract. While the provided snippets don't directly state that 'shall' is mandatory, the Law Explanation highlights the legally binding nature of contractual obligations. This alteration directly contradicts the principle that parties are bound by their agreements, undermining the enforceability of the contract.

perturbation.original_text: 12.1 Any disputes between the Parties arising from the performance of this Agreement shall be resolved through amicable negotiations; in case such negotiations fail, both Parties agree to submit the dispute to the competent court where Party A is domiciled.
perturbation.changed_text: 12.1 Any disputes between the Parties arising from the performance of this Agreement may be resolved through amicable negotiations; in case such negotiations fail, both Parties may consider submitting the dispute to any court they deem appropriate.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall' to 'may' and removing the jurisdiction clause undermines the certainty of dispute resolution.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Civil Procedure Law of the People's Republic of China, Article 34
perturbation.law_citation: Civil Procedure Law of the People's Republic of China, Article 34
perturbation.law_explanation: Article 34 of the Civil Procedure Law of the People's Republic of China addresses the agreement on jurisdiction for contract disputes. The original text designated a specific court, but the modified text eliminates the mandatory submission and defined jurisdiction. This weakens the enforceability of the jurisdiction clause as it makes the court choice optional. It introduces ambiguity, which is contradictory to the need of a clear and legally binding agreement.

perturbation.justification: The modified text weakens the dispute resolution mechanism, which undermines the certainty and enforceability of the jurisdiction clause. The original text mandated submission to a specific court, but the changed text makes court selection optional, potentially leading to ambiguity. Although there are no direct snippets to reference, the law explanation points to Article 34 of the Civil Procedure Law of the People's Republic of China, which emphasizes the importance of clear and legally binding agreements, which the modified text contradicts.
